tr: Masanın üzerinde üç bardak su var.
en: There are three glasses of water on the table.

tr: Evimizin önünde pahalı bir araba var.
en: There is an expensive car in front of our house.

tr: Masanın altında 2 tane sevimli kedi var.
en: There are two cute cats under the table.
How much money is there in your pocket?
How many children do you have?
I have no money in the bank. / I don't have any money in the bank

tr: Denizin dibinde birkaç tane köpekbalığı var.
en: There are some sharks below the sea.

tr: Senin sınıfında kaç tane öğrenci var?
en: How many students are there in your class?

tr: Evimizin yakınlarında çok güzel bir göl var.
en: There is a very beautiful lake near our house.
There are no people on the streets today. / There isn't any people on the streets today

tr: Elimde biraz para var. Yeni bir bilgisayar satın almak istiyorum.
en: There is some money in my hand / I have some money in my hand. I want to buy a new computer.

tr: Ayşe’nin bizim için hiç vakti yok.
en: Ayşe has no time for us. / Ayşe doesn't have any time for us
Ali has no money in the bank. / Ali doesn't have any money in the bank
There are crazy questions in my mind.
There are no happy people in Turkey. / There aren't any happy people in Turkey
My cat has small ears.
They have no friends at school. / They don't have any friends at school
The people in Turkey have important problems.
There are powerful countries behind Turkey.
There is some milk in my glass.
Ali is my best friend.

tr: Daha iyi bir hayat için daha iyi bir işe ihtiyacım var.
en: I need a better job for a better life.

tr: Benim babam dünyadaki en güçlü adamdır.
en: My father is the strongest man in the world.
Ayşe's phone is more expensive than mine.

tr: Yaşamak için daha çok paraya ihtiyacımız var.
en: We need more money to live.
This was the worst day of my life.

tr: Çocuklarım için daha iyi bir gelecek istiyorum.
en: I want a better future for my children.

tr: Daha uzun bir hayat için daha sağlıklı besinler yemelisin.
en: You must eat healthier food for a longer life.

tr: Ali bizim sınıftaki en kısa boylu öğrencidir.
en: Ali is the shortest student in our class.

tr: İngilizce öğrenmek için daha çok çalışman lazım.
en: You need to study more to learn English.

tr: Dünyadaki en yaşlı insanlar Japonya’da yaşıyor.
en: The oldest people in the world live in Japan.
Everyone wants to be richer.

tr: Garsondan daha sıcak bir kahve istedim.
en: I wanted a hotter coffee from the waiter / I asked the waiter for a hotter coffee.

tr: Türkiye’nin en yüksek dağı Ağrı Dağı’dır.
en: The highest mountain of Turkey is Mount Ararat.
America is the richest country in the world.
I studied a lot to learn English.

tr: Uzun bir günün ardından en iyi arkadaşımla birlikte kahve içiyoruz.
en: We are drinking coffee with my best friend after a long day.
We are staying in the biggest room in the hotel.
We need to be stronger.
English is easier than Turkish.

tr: Senin daha iyi bir öğrenci olmanı istiyorum.
en: I want you to be a better student.

tr: Çocukları okuldan almak için oraya gittim.
en: I went there to take the children from school.
I will meet Ayşe at 9 o'clock tomorrow.
You have to study to be successful.
I won't be there next week.
I couldn't finish school because of them.
You should come with us.
I must call my mother today.
I can't answer these questions.
It might rain tomorrow
The children may not play on the grass!
If I were in Germany, I would be rich.
If you listened to me, you would be successful.
Ali said (that) he wanted to come with us.
I will be with you tomorrow.
We will be on holiday next week.

tr: Gelecek yıl yeni bir araba satın alacağım.
en: I will buy a new car next year.
I sent the emails to my manager yesterday.
We weren't here last week.

tr: Ali on yıl evvel çok başarılı bir öğrenciydi.
en: Ali was a very successful student ten years ago.
My wife asked me to take the children to school. / My wife wanted me to take the children to school.
My wife asked me to take the children from school. / My wife wanted me to take the children from school

tr: Benim Ankara’da çok iyi arkadaşlarım vardı.
en: I had very good friends in Ankara.
When did you finish school?

tr: Ailemle beraber yeni bir hayata başlamak için İstanbul’dan taşındık.
en: We moved from Istanbul to start a new life with my family.
There was no food at home. / There isn't any food at home

tr: Ali dün çocukları için yeni bir bilgisayar satın aldı.
en: Ali bought a new computer for his children yesterday.

tr: Yeni telefonumu arkadaşlarıma göstermek için telefonumu okula getirdim.
en: I brought my phone to school to show my new phone to my friends.

tr: Dün müdürümden çok önemli bir email aldım.
en: I received a very important email from my manager yesterday.

tr: Senin iyi bir öğrenci olduğunu biliyordum.
en: I knew that you were a good student.

tr: Ayşe’ye bana biraz para vermesini söyledim.
en: I told Ayşe to give me some money.

tr: Öğrencilerime İngilizcenin çok kolay olduğunu söyledim.
en: I told my students that English was very easy.

tr: Geçen hafta parkta çok fakir bir adam gördüm.
en: I saw a very poor man in the park last week.
I won't answer your questions.
I used my key to open the door.

tr: Geçen yaz Antalya’da her gün yüzdük.
en: We swam every day in Antalya last summer.
I drank ten glasses of water yesterday.
When did you finish university?
Will you be here tomorrow?

tr: Geçen yıl tatilde kaç para harcadın?
en: How much money did you spend on holiday last year?
Why did you eat my food?

tr: Sen Ankara’ya gitmek için kimin arabasını kullandın?
en: Whose car did you use to go to Ankara?

tr: Sınavda kolay sorular sordum ama Ali bu soruları cevaplayamadı.
en: I asked easy questions in the exam but Ali couldn't answer them.

tr: Yaşlı bir adam için kapıyı açtım.
en: I opened the door for an old man.

tr: Dün üniversiteden arkadaşlarımla küçük bir cafede buluştum.
en: I met with my friends from university in a small cafe yesterday.

tr: Sen geçen hafta Ayşeyle nerede karşılaştın?
en: Where did you meet Ayşe last week?
We won't be here next year.

tr: İyi bir işe sahip olmak için, İngilizce konuşmak zorundasın.
en: You have to speak English to have a good job,

tr: Bence sen başarılı bir öğrenci olabilirsin.
en: I think (that) you can be a successful student.
I don't agree with you.
Where did you stay on holiday last year?
The doctor told me to take off my clothes.

tr: Dün paltomu giydim çünkü hava çok soğuktu.
en: I wore my coat yesterday because the weather was very cold.
Why did you leave me?
I think (that) I lost my phone.

tr: İki gün önce sokakta bir sürü para buldum.
en: I found a lot of money on the street two days ago.

tr: Biz, daha iyi bir hayat için savaştık.
en: We fought for a better life.
I was born in İstanbul on the 8th of October 1972
What time did you wake up today?

tr: Pazar günü kahvaltıda çocuklarım için bir omlet yaptım.
en: I made an omelette for my children at breakfast on Sunday.
